Scenario.title: Energy Sector Cyber Attack Scenario
Scenario.overview: Energy Complex A sophisticated cyber attack targeting the energy sector, utilizing various tactics and techniques to achieve initial access, persistence, privilege escalation, defense evasion, credential access, discovery, lateral movement, collection, exfiltration, and impact.

threat_actor_profile.motivation: Gather information about the energy company's infrastructure

Scenario.attack_surface: AVAILABLE_SURFACES: ["Device theft", "Weak passwords", "Improper configuration", "Vulnerability", "Internet-facing assets", "Shared DB or directory", "Obsolete products", "Injection flaws", "Custom APIs", "Autonomous system numbers(ASNs)", "SSL Certificates and Attribution", "WHOIS records, contacts and history", "TCP/IP ports", "Public and private cloud", "Web server services such as emails and databases", "Shadow IT", "Poor implementation of access controls", "Malicious insiders", "Social engineering", "Insufficient physical security controls", "Authentication issues"]
Scenario.attack_vector: AVAILABLE_VECTORS: ["External/Removable media", "Loss or Theft of equipment", "Attrition", "Web", "Account takeover", "Inadequate encryption", "Browser-based attack", "Application compromise", "Open port", "Vulnerability exploit", "Ransomware", "Malware", "Man in The Middle(MiTM)", "Session hijacking", "Brute-force attack", "Distributed Denial of Service(DDoS)", "Watering-hole", "Compromised and weak credentials", "SIM Swap", "Security misconfigurations and vulnerabilities", "Web forms", "IP address and IP blocks", "Domains and subdomains", "Botnet", "Credential compromise", "Telephony Denial of Service(TDoS)", "Replay Attack", "Cryptanalysis Attacks", "Side Channel Attacks", "Hardware Trojans", "Covert Attacks", "Sleep Deprivation Attack", "Clock Skewing", "Attacks Targeting System Reputation", "Data and Command Integrity Attack", "Eavesdropping", "Command and Control Attacks", "Phishing", "Impersonations/Spoofing", "Improper usage", "Dumpster diving", "Advanced Persistent Threat(APT)", "Baiting", "Shoulder surfing", "Reverse social engineering", "Business Email Compromise(BEC)", "Advanced Fee", "Extortion"]

stage.name: Reconnaissance
stage.description: The attacker gathers information about the energy company's infrastructure through open-source intelligence.

stage.name: Reconnaissance
stage.description: The attacker identifies open ports and services on the energy company's network through network scanning.

stage.name: Resource Development
stage.description: The attacker creates malware to be used for initial access.

stage.name: Initial Access
stage.description: The attacker tricks an employee into opening the malware via email.

stage.name: Execution
stage.description: The attacker injects the malware into the system.

stage.name: Persistence
stage.description: The attacker schedules the malware to run at startup.

stage.name: Privilege Escalation
stage.description: The attacker gains elevated privileges using a known vulnerability.

stage.name: Defense Evasion
stage.description: The attacker disables anti-virus software to avoid detection.

stage.name: Credential Access
stage.description: The attacker cracks passwords to gain access to sensitive systems.

stage.name: Discovery
stage.description: The attacker monitors network traffic for valuable data.

stage.name: Lateral Movement
stage.description: The attacker uses remote services to move laterally through the network.

stage.name: Collection
stage.description: The attacker collects sensitive data from local systems.

stage.name: Exfiltration
stage.description: The attacker exfiltrates data over alternative protocols.

stage.name: Impact
stage.description: The attacker disrupts operations by causing a denial of service.

Scenario.summary: A complex cyber attack scenario involving multiple tactics and techniques to compromise the energy sector.
